plan.contextChunk:
can use the equation of a line in homogeneous coordinates,
$\boldsymbol{l}^\mathsf{T}\mathbf{p}=0$. From equation
(@eq-homography_generic), we have that
$\mathbf{p} = \mathbf{H}^{-1} \mathbf{p}'$. Replacing the last equality
into the equation of the line gives
$(\mathbf{H} ^{-\mathsf{T}}\boldsymbol{l})^\mathsf{T}\mathbf{p'}=0$,
which corresponds to the equation of the line for the projected points
$\mathbf{p}'$.

In a general homography, angles and lengths are not preserved.
Therefore, parallel lines might not remain parallel, and lines of
identical length might have different lengths after a homography.
@fig-illustration_homography2 shows how a planar grid gets transformed
after applying a homography.

![Homography. Colinear points remain colinear after transformation. Angles between lines are not preserved.](figures/homography/example_homography.png){width="70%" #fig-illustration_homography2}

Let's start discussing in which scenarios the coordinates of points
between to images are related by a homography.

### Camera Rotation

Consider the following three images shown in @fig-example_three_images.

:::{#fig-example_three_images layout-ncol="3"}
![](figures/homography/3_IMG_7883_small.jpg){width="52%"}

![](figures/homography/2_IMG_7884_small.jpg){width="30%"}

![](figures/homography/1_IMG_7885_small.jpg){width="30%"}

elements: lines from corresponding corners between grids

interactions.teaches: Allows inspection of how the 3D perspective distorts the grid, demonstrating the effects of homography on angles and parallel lines.

interactions.label: Apply Homography
interactions.teaches: Toggles the homography transformation, showing how the grid is initially undistorted then transformed to illustrate the concept.

demo_steps.title: Initial Grid Setup
demo_steps.narration: Here is the basic 2D grid. Notice all lines are parallel and lengths are uniform across the grid.

demo_steps.title: Applying Homography
demo_steps.narration: As the homography is applied, observe how the grid transforms. Parallel lines may no longer be parallel, and angles can change, illustrating the typical impact of homographic transformations.

demo_steps.title: Inspecting Transform Effects
demo_steps.narration: Rotate the view to examine how the grid appears in this 3D perspective, emphasizing the non-preserved geometric properties after a homography.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Initial view directly facing the left grid, then transitioning to side view to emphasize transformation depth.

inline_constraints.default_view: must match the original image silhouette, size, label scale, and crop
inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most 2 compact sliders/toggles, visible near an edge with no filled panel, and must not cover important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

interactionPlan.notes: Use Three.js to smoothly transition between 2D grid and 3D projection. Maintain grid alignment and line clarity throughout interactions. No additional overlays beyond described transformations.

discrepancies: The grids are shown in perspective rather than as flat 2D and transformed 3D views.
The arrow indicating transformation is not visually centered between the grids.
Lines connecting grid corners are misaligned in the 3D view.
Grid rotation and orientation seem incorrect.
evaluation.notes: The perspective and orientation of the grids are incorrect, with missing key interactions, causing misalignment.
action_items: Correct the perspective to match a flat front-on view for the 2D grid and a correct 3D perspective for the transformed grid.
Re-align the connection lines between corresponding corners in both grids.
Ensure the arrow is accurately placed and represents the transformation process more accurately.
